title: Fix calendar view sizing in DashboardPage to be properly visible and well-fitted

requirements.text: Resize and reformat the calendar view in DashboardPage so it fits properly within the screen without overflowing or being clipped. The calendar grid must be compact, fully visible, and well-proportioned on both mobile and desktop viewport sizes. Ensure the calendar container uses constrained max-width, appropriate font sizes for day labels and date numbers, and sufficient but not excessive padding/margins so no part of the calendar is hidden or cut off. The calendar should remain styled consistently with the app's terracotta/cream theme.

quotes: calenedar view to shring make proper visiable

acceptanceCriteria: The calendar in DashboardPage is fully visible within the viewport on both mobile and desktop screen sizes.
No calendar cells, day headers, or navigation controls are clipped or overflow the screen.
The calendar appears compact and well-proportioned — not oversized or too spread out.
Day labels and date numbers are legible at appropriate font sizes.
The calendar container has a constrained max-width so it does not span the full page width unnecessarily.
Styling remains consistent with the app's terracotta (#C47A4A), dark-brown (#2A1A0E), and cream (#FAF5EE) color palette.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the existing date storage or parsing logic — the off-by-one fix already applied must remain intact.
Do not alter any backend logic.
nonGoals: Changing the calendar's date selection or jap count display logic.
Redesigning any page other than the DashboardPage calendar section.
Adding new calendar features or interactions.
